speaker: RUMOR
text: Open your ears, for which of you will stop

speaker: RUMOR
text: The vent of hearing when loud Rumor speaks?

speaker: RUMOR
text: I, from the orient to the drooping west,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Making the wind my post-horse, still unfold

speaker: RUMOR
text: The acts commencèd on this ball of earth.

speaker: RUMOR
text: Upon my tongues continual slanders ride,

speaker: RUMOR
text: The which in every language I pronounce,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Stuffing the ears of men with false reports.

speaker: RUMOR
text: I speak of peace while covert enmity

speaker: RUMOR
text: Under the smile of safety wounds the world.

speaker: RUMOR
text: And who but Rumor, who but only I,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Make fearful musters and prepared defense

speaker: RUMOR
text: Whiles the big year, swoll’n with some other grief,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Is thought with child by the stern tyrant war,

speaker: RUMOR
text: And no such matter? Rumor is a pipe

speaker: RUMOR
text: Blown by surmises, jealousies, conjectures,

speaker: RUMOR
text: And of so easy and so plain a stop

speaker: RUMOR
text: That the blunt monster with uncounted heads,

speaker: RUMOR
text: The still-discordant wav’ring multitude,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Can play upon it. But what need I thus

speaker: RUMOR
text: My well-known body to anatomize

speaker: RUMOR
text: Among my household? Why is Rumor here?

speaker: RUMOR
text: I run before King Harry’s victory,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Who in a bloody field by Shrewsbury

speaker: RUMOR
text: Hath beaten down young Hotspur and his troops,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Quenching the flame of bold rebellion

speaker: RUMOR
text: Even with the rebels’ blood. But what mean I

speaker: RUMOR
text: To speak so true at first? My office is

speaker: RUMOR
text: To noise abroad that Harry Monmouth fell

speaker: RUMOR
text: Under the wrath of noble Hotspur’s sword,

speaker: RUMOR
text: And that the King before the Douglas’ rage

speaker: RUMOR
text: Stooped his anointed head as low as death.

speaker: RUMOR
text: This have I rumored through the peasant towns

speaker: RUMOR
text: Between that royal field of Shrewsbury

speaker: RUMOR
text: And this worm-eaten hold of ragged stone,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Where Hotspur’s father, old Northumberland,

speaker: RUMOR
text: Lies crafty-sick. The posts come tiring on,

speaker: RUMOR
text: And not a man of them brings other news

speaker: RUMOR
text: Than they have learnt of me. From Rumor’s tongues

speaker: RUMOR
text: They bring smooth comforts false, worse than true wrongs.
